require-stew-stove: Must be placed on a stew stove.
eternalstew:handbook-eternalstew-text:
Eternal Stew is inspired by real-world perpetual stews that simmer for months or even years, with new ingredients added over time.

How it works: There are no slots or ratios—just add raw food directly from your hotbar into the hot pot. Everything you add is cooked into a shared stew. Serve into fired clay bowls; each bowl inherits nutrition from the current stew. Broth is only water for now.

Stove: Place and fuel the Eternal Stew Stove like a simple clay oven. Light it with a torch. It burns faster to reach simmer (~95°C) and then slows its burn rate in the kept-warm state (~60°C+).

Pot: Place the Eternal Stew Pot on top of the stove. You need 10 L of water to start a batch. Each bowl served consumes broth; keep topping up. If broth gets low, you’ll need to add more water before cooking more ingredients.

States: Raw → Simmering → Kept Warm → Cooling. While kept warm, servings never spoil. If the pot cools, servings slowly perish until reheated or emptied. Keeping it warm for long stretches rewards you with a steady supply of bowls on demand.
eternalstew:handbook-stove-text: Foundation heat source for the Eternal Stew Pot. Place and light like a clay oven; orientation follows player facing. Add fuel (firewood/coal), light with a torch, and keep it above ~95°C to simmer a fresh batch. Once the pot is cooked, maintaining ~60°C+ enters kept-warm and reduces fuel burn.
eternalstew:handbook-pot-text: Sits on the Eternal Stew Stove. Needs 10 L of water to start cooking; more can be added later. Add raw food while hot—no slots or ratios required. While kept warm, servings do not spoil; if it cools, servings slowly perish. Each bowl served consumes broth; refill water when it runs low. Serve with fired clay bowls directly from the pot; bowls inherit the stew’s nutrition.
cfg-keepwarm-burn-comment: Burn rate multiplier while kept warm (vanilla = 1.0).
cfg-max-fuel-comment: Maximum number of fuel pieces the stove can hold.
cfg-max-ingredients-comment: Maximum ingredient items waiting to cook in the pot.
cfg-simmer-duration-comment: Multiplier on simmer time (lower = faster cooking).
